5. Fill in Name field
6. Click 'Create'

2. Organization created and found in contacts list

Title: Search Contact with Wildcard Operator
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), multiple contacts exist with varied names
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate the search bar
3. Enter a search term using the asterisk wildcard (e.g. John*)
4. Press Enter or click Search
5. Verify the results include all contacts whose names start with the prefix
6. Try a mid-string wildcard (e.g. *son) and verify trailing match behavior
7. Verify results are limited to relevant matches only
Expected Result:
- Wildcard (*) operator is supported in the contact search field
- Search with prefix wildcard returns all matching contacts
- Mid-string wildcard returns contacts with the suffix in the name
- Non-matching wildcard queries return an empty result set
- Search results update without page reload

Title: Search Contact with Replace Operator
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), contacts exist that differ by a single character in their names
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Enter a search term using the question mark replace operator (e.g. Jo?n)
3. Press Enter or click Search
4. Verify results include contacts matching the single-character wildcard
5. Try ? at different positions in the query
6. Verify non-matching queries return empty results
Expected Result:
- Question mark (?) operator is supported in the contact search field
- Single-character wildcard matches exactly one character at its position
- Results include contacts like John, Joan matching Jo?n pattern
- Pattern matching is case-insensitive
- Empty result set is displayed when no contacts match the pattern

Title: Contact Filters UI
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), contacts list has multiple entries with different statuses and organizations
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate the filter controls (column headers or filter panel)
3. Apply a filter on the Status column (e.g. Active)
4. Verify only contacts with Active status are shown
5. Apply an additional filter on Organization
6. Verify results are filtered by both criteria simultaneously
7. Clear all filters and verify the full list is restored
Expected Result:
- Filter controls are visible on the contacts list
- Status filter narrows the list to matching contacts
- Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously
- Combined filters produce a correctly intersected result
- Clearing filters restores the full unfiltered list
- Filter state is maintained during pagination

Title: Email Verification After Account Creation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), email service is configured for the test environment
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Click Add to create a new contact
3. Enter contact details including a valid test email address
4. Save the contact record
5. Optionally create a login account for the contact
6. Verify a verification email is sent to the provided address
7. Check that the account status reflects pending verification
Expected Result:
- Contact is created successfully with the provided email
- System sends a verification email to the contact address after account creation
- Account status shows as pending verification or unverified until email is confirmed
- Verification email contains a valid verification link
- Resend verification option is available in the admin UI

Title: Contact About Field

Title: xAPI Contact Query with About Field
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), a contact with a populated About field exists, xAPI endpoint is accessible
Steps:
1. Open a contact in admin and note the About field value
2. Open the GraphQL xAPI explorer or use a REST client
3. Execute a contact query by ID or email
4. Include the about field in the query selection
5. Verify the response includes the about field with the correct value
6. Update the About field in admin and re-query via xAPI
7. Verify the updated value is returned
Expected Result:
- xAPI contact query returns the about field in the response
- About field value matches what is stored in admin
- Updated About field value is reflected in subsequent xAPI queries
- Field is correctly typed (string) in the response schema
- Query works for contacts with empty About field (returns null or empty string)

Title: Role Assignment in Customer Module

Expected Result:

Title: Multi-Org Contact Hierarchy
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), two separate organizations exist, a contact is a member of one organization only
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate a contact assigned to Organization A
3. Verify the contact appears in Organization A member list
4. Navigate to Organization B
5. Verify the contact does not appear in Organization B member list
6. Add the contact to Organization B via the member management UI
7. Verify the contact now appears in both Organization A and B
8. Remove the contact from Organization A and verify they remain in Organization B only
Expected Result:
- Contact visibility is scoped to the organizations they belong to
- A contact not in Organization B is not visible in its member list
- Contact can be added to multiple organizations
- Removing a contact from one organization does not affect membership in others
- Organization hierarchy is correctly maintained for reporting and access control
